subject: [PATCH v4 5/5] mm/memcontrol: remove unused memcg_stock code

2026-06-23.patch_summary: This patch removes unused code related to handling 'memcg_stock' in the Linux kernel's memory control (mm/memcontrol) module. The removal is possible because all relevant logic has been moved to the 'page_counter_stock' module. The patch preserves the 'FLUSHING_CACHED_CHARGE' flag, which is still needed by another part of the code. The changes involve deleting 186 lines of code from a single file (mm/memcontrol.c).
